intro: The clearest split is that Bonsai has a free tier while Coveo does not. Bonsai is Managed Elasticsearch and OpenSearch hosting with automated backups and monitoring; Coveo is AI-powered search and recommendations platform for enterprise portals, e-commerce, and support sites. The data points Bonsai at e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search, and Coveo at e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search. Pricing: Bonsai is freemium from $0/mo; Coveo is subscription. Lock-in is low for Bonsai and high for Coveo, with DX at 4/5 vs 4/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 2/5. On listed strengths, Bonsai emphasises e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search, and Coveo emphasises e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits simple projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Bonsai is for e-commerce; Coveo is for e-commerce; decide based on whether a free tier matters.
verdict_a: Choose Bonsai if you want to start on a free tier before paying and your workload matches e-commerce. Skip it if your context is simple projects.
verdict_b: Choose Coveo if starting on a paid subscription upfront is acceptable and your workload matches e-commerce. Skip it if your context is simple projects.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as $0/mo for Bonsai and unknown for Coveo. Bonsai offers a free tier while Coveo does not, so initial cost favors Bonsai. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Bonsai to Coveo?
faq.a: Bonsai is tagged with low lock-in (managed elasticsearch, standard index). Coveo is tagged with high lock-in (enterprise search platform, hard to migrate). At least one side is high lock-in, so budget migration time for re-implementing integrations.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Bonsai or Coveo?
faq.a: Both are rated 4/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Coveo a good alternative to Bonsai?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches e-commerce, since both list the same fit. Coveo is flagged as not for simple projects, so confirm your context does not fall there. Note that moving from Bonsai to Coveo means leaving a free tier behind.
